header: ML-KEM-768 per FIPS 203 with KeyGen / Encapsulate / Decapsulate KATs and implicit-rejection tests. Wycheproof skips this parameter set; Tadashi covers it specifically.

Tests store seeds (D, Z) and message rather than the full encapsulation key (~1184 bytes) so runners regenerate keys from seeds. Each test exercises one operation; KeyGen tests publish SHA3-256(ek) and SHA3-256(dk) for cross-impl verification.

Decapsulate.description: Decapsulate(dk, ct) recovers the shared secret.

RoundTrip.description: Decapsulate of Encapsulate output recovers the same shared.

ImplicitRejection.description: Tampered or random ct: FO re-encryption check fails; spec mandates returning SHAKE256(Z || ct) instead of an error or zero output.
ImplicitRejection.effect: Bug if impl returns zero, errors out, or returns the raw KPrime on FO mismatch.

FOReencryption.description: Decapsulate re-encrypts mPrime under the embedded public key and constant-time-compares against the input ct.

RandomCiphertext.description: Ciphertext is not derived from any Encapsulate; FO check rejects.

CrossKey.description: Ciphertext was produced for a different key pair.

BitFlip.description: A single bit of the ciphertext was modified.

DZSeparation.description: D and Z are distinct; catches impls that conflate the two 32-byte seeds.

NonCanonicalEk.description: Public key has a 12-bit coefficient >= q (= 3329). FIPS 203 Algorithm 17 step 1 requires rejection.

InputValidation.description: Input fails a structural or canonical-encoding check that the spec mandates.

DegenerateKey.description: Public key is mathematically valid (canonical encoding) but degenerate (e.g. all-zero coefficients); accepting is correct per spec but offers no security in practice.

DkValidation.description: FIPS 203 Algorithm 24 (dkCheck) defines an optional integrity check on dk. Implementations may choose to recompute H(ek) on Decaps (strict) or trust the stored H (permissive). Both are spec-compliant.
DkValidation.effect: Strict impls reject; permissive impls produce a garbage shared.

HFieldTamper.description: The H(ek) field embedded in dk has been modified.

Acceptable.description: Either matching the recorded output or rejecting cleanly is a spec-conformant choice.

UnluckySample.description: Seed bruteforced so that SampleNTT must consume an unusually large number of XOF bytes (probability ~2^-38 per matrix entry).
UnluckySample.effect: Catches impls with fixed-size XOF buffers that overflow on the unlucky path (DJB calls the worst-case form KyberBleed).

XofBufferOverflow.description: Fixed-size XOF read buffer too small for the unlucky tail of SampleNTT; can leak adjacent state.

StrcmpVsMemcmp.description: Ciphertext crafted so that the FO re-encryption check has matching null-byte prefixes; an impl using strcmp short-circuits and skips implicit rejection.
StrcmpVsMemcmp.effect: Yields wrong K on the strcmp path; correct K on memcmp.

HexByteOrder.description: Bytes are asymmetric; reversed byte order would produce a different result.

HexNibbleOrder.description: Each byte has distinct high and low nibbles.

Pseudorandom.description: Standard correctness check on output value.

tests.comment: ML-KEM-768 KeyGen with all-zero seeds (D = Z = 0^32). Tests the full KeyGen path on the zero-input edge.
ML-KEM-768 KeyGen with incremental seeds (D = 0..31, Z = 32..63). Matches @noble/post-quantum seed convention where the 64-byte input is D || Z.
ML-KEM-768 KeyGen with all-0xff seeds. Stresses the SHAKE expansion of rho with the maximum-byte input.
ML-KEM-768 KeyGen with asymmetric D and Z (different patterns). Catches D/Z mix-up bugs.
C2SP/CCTV unluckysample for ML-KEM-768: seed forces SampleNTT to read >575 bytes from SHAKE-128 (probability ~2^-38). Catches fixed-size XOF buffer overflow; DJB calls the worst-case 'KyberBleed'.
ML-KEM-768 Encapsulate with zero seeds and zero message. Tests the full Encapsulate path on the all-zero input edge.
ML-KEM-768 Encapsulate: incremental seeds + zero message.
ML-KEM-768 Encapsulate: incremental seeds + incremental message.
ML-KEM-768 Encapsulate: incremental seeds + all-0xff message. Bug-catching for messages with all bits set.
ML-KEM-768 Encapsulate with asymmetric message bytes; nibbles within each byte differ.

Encapsulate with a non-canonical public key: first 12-bit coefficient set to q (= 3329). FIPS 203 Algorithm 17 step 1 requires rejecting ek encodings where any coefficient >= q. Both rbx-crypto and noble reject (via different error messages); impl-bug if impl accepts and processes.
Encapsulate with a non-canonical public key: first 12-bit coefficient set to 4095 (= 2^12 - 1, the maximum value representable in 12 bits, far above q). Must reject.

Encapsulate with an all-zero public key (all coefficients = 0, all of rho = 0). Canonical encoding (0 < q) but degenerate; both impls accept. No security in practice — an adversary with the matching all-zero dk-stand-in could decapsulate. Tadashi records the deterministic output for cross-impl agreement.
ML-KEM-768 Decapsulate: round-trip with the all-zero KeyGen + all-zero Encapsulate. Shared must equal Encapsulate's output.
ML-KEM-768 Decapsulate: round-trip with incremental KeyGen + incremental message.

ML-KEM-768 Decapsulate: bit 0 of ciphertext flipped, then decapsulated. The FO re-encryption check fails; output is the implicit-rejection value = SHAKE256(Z || ct'). MUST differ from the original shared (catches impls that return zero or the raw KPrime on FO mismatch).
ML-KEM-768 Decapsulate: bit flipped near the middle of ciphertext. Same implicit-rejection path.
ML-KEM-768 Decapsulate: pseudorandom ciphertext (not derived from any Encapsulate). Implicit rejection path.
ML-KEM-768 Decapsulate: dk from incremental seeds, ct from all-0xff seeds. Cross-key forgery; implicit rejection.

C2SP/CCTV strcmp vector for ML-KEM-768: ciphertext crafted so that the FO re-encryption check has matching null-byte prefixes. If Decaps uses strcmp instead of memcmp the comparison short-circuits and the implicit-rejection path is skipped, yielding the wrong K.

Decapsulate with dk whose embedded H(ek) field has bit 7 of byte 0 flipped. FIPS 203 Algorithm 24 (dkCheck) defines an optional check that the stored H equals SHA3-256(stored ek); Algorithm 19 (Decaps) itself does not require it. rbx-crypto silently uses the corrupted H field and produces a different (garbage) shared; @noble/post-quantum rejects via 'hash check failed'. Both behaviors are spec-compliant.
